game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: Piano Game: Players tap black tiles as they scroll down the screen in sync with the rhythm of a song, while avoiding white tiles, to simulate playing a piano. The core gameplay requires precise timing; correctly tapping a black tile produces a musical note and continues the combo, while missing a black tile or tapping a white tile results in a "miss" and can end the song.
game_info.description: Magic Tiles is a rhythm-based game where you must tap black tiles as they scroll down the screen while avoiding white tiles. Each correctly tapped black tile awards points and continues your combo. Miss a black tile or tap a white tile, and you lose. The game progressively gets faster, challenging your reflexes and timing. How long can you keep up with the increasing difficulty?
game_info.controls:
DOWN ARROW: Tap the leftmost lane (column 1)
LEFT ARROW: Tap the second lane from left (column 2)
UP ARROW: Tap the third lane from left (column 3)
RIGHT ARROW: Tap the rightmost lane (column 4)
ENTER: Start the game from the start screen
ESC: Pause/unpause during gameplay
R: Restart the game (works on game over screen)
game_info.automated_testing:
<TEST_1>
<test_description>Tests basic game functionality by ensuring the game compiles without errors and can be played using sticky keys that change periodically.</test_description>
<strategy_description>The test will press keys corresponding to the lanes with black tiles as they approach the bottom of the screen, using a randomized pattern to simulate player input.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>The game should run without errors, respond to key inputs, and demonstrate that the basic gameplay mechanics work as intended.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_1>
<TEST_2>
<test_description>Tests that the game can be won by reaching a high score threshold through perfect play.</test_description>
<strategy_description>The test will identify black tiles in each lane and press the corresponding keys with precise timing to maximize score and maintain combos.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>The player should reach the win condition score threshold without missing any tiles, demonstrating that winning is possible with good gameplay.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_2>
<TEST_3>
<test_description>Tests the game's difficulty progression by playing through multiple difficulty levels.</test_description>
<strategy_description>The test will play accurately through initial levels and verify that the game speed increases at appropriate score thresholds.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>The game should demonstrate increasing difficulty with tiles moving faster as the player's score increases, showing that the difficulty progression system works.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_3>
<TEST_4>
<test_description>Tests the combo system by maintaining an unbroken streak of correctly tapped tiles.</test_description>
<strategy_description>The test will focus on hitting consecutive black tiles to build and maintain a high combo multiplier.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>The combo counter should increase with each successful hit, and the score should reflect the combo multiplier, verifying that the combo system functions correctly.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_4>
<TEST_5>
<test_description>Tests the game's loss condition by deliberately missing a black tile.</test_description>
<strategy_description>The test will play normally until a specific point, then deliberately let a black tile pass without tapping it.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>The game should transition to the GAME_OVER_LOSE state when a black tile is missed, confirming that the loss condition works as expected.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_5>
